signals.short_reason: 경기 회복 기대, 투자 심리 개선
signals.summary: 오늘 뉴욕 증시의 S&P 500 지수는 전반적인 강세를 나타내며 투자 심리 회복을 명확히 보여주었습니다. 시장 참여자들은 견조한 경제 지표와 기업들의 긍정적인 실적 전망에 힘입어 위험 자산 선호 심리를 강화하는 모습입니다. 특히, 거시 경제 전반에 걸친 회복 기대감이 지수 상승의 주요 동력으로 작용했으며, 이는 특정 산업군에 국한되지 않고 폭넓은 매수세로 이어졌습니다. 이러한 흐름은 당분간 시장의 활력을 유지하는 데 기여할 것으로 예상됩니다.

news_articles.title: S&P 500 Market Analysis

signals.summary: 오늘의 나스닥 시장은 전반적인 시장 흐름 속에서 개별 종목들의 움직임에 초점이 맞춰진 분석이 주를 이루었습니다. 투자자들은 현재 시장의 주요 동인과 개별 기술주들의 잠재적 성장 동력을 면밀히 살피며 향후 방향성을 탐색하는 모습입니다. 전반적인 시황의 영향과 더불어, 특정 종목들의 실적 발표나 기술적 모멘텀이 시장 심리에 중요한 변수로 작용하고 있습니다.

signals.short_reason: 메타 전력 선구매 계약, 52주 고점 대비 급락
signals.summary: 원자력 기술 기업인 오클로(Oklo, NYSE:OKLO)는 최근 52주 최고가 대비 67% 하락한 상황에서 투자자들의 주목을 받고 있습니다. 특히, 오클로는 계획 중인 오하이오 원자력 발전소의 전력을 메타 플랫폼스(Meta Platforms)에 선구매하는 계약을 체결하며 초기 프로젝트 자금 확보에 긍정적인 진전을 보였습니다. 이 계약은 2026년 예정된 부지 특성화 작업 등 초기 단계 자금 조달을 지원하며, 데이터 센터의 미래 에너지 수요와 소형 모듈 원자로(SMR) 개발을 직접적으로 연결시켰습니다. 투자자들은 이제 오클로가 허가 절차, 건설 준비 태세, 그리고 향후 추가적인 전력 구매 계약을 어떻게 이행하는지에 초점을 맞추고 있습니다. 또한, 회사는 2025년 연간 실적 발표 및 사업 업데이트를 3월 17일로 예정하고 있어, 향후 재무 성과와 전략적 방향에 대한 구체적인 내용이 기대됩니다. 전반적으로 주가 하락세 속에서 메타와의 계약이라는 강력한 모멘텀이 발생했으나, 실제 프로젝트 실행력이 핵심 관건입니다.

news_articles.title: Oklo Meta Deal Links Early Nuclear Funding To Future Data Center Demand
news_articles.content:
Oklo Meta Deal Links Early Nuclear Funding To Future Data Center Demand
Simply Wall St
Wed, March 4, 2026 at 4:17 PM MST
with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.
Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) has signed a prepayment power agreement with Meta Platforms for electricity from its planned Ohio nuclear plant.
The arrangement is intended to fund the initial stages of the project, including pre construction and site characterization work targeted for 2026.
The deal links Oklo's planned small modular reactor output to future energy needs for Meta's operations, including data centers.
Oklo is developing small modular nuclear reactors aimed at providing reliable, carbon free power, and this agreement with Meta puts a tech company directly in the customer seat. For investors, it ties a young nuclear developer to the growing electricity demand from data centers and AI infrastructure. It also gives NYSE:OKLO a defined commercial pathway for part of its planned Ohio capacity.
Looking ahead, the prepayment structure may help Oklo address early project costs and reduce some funding uncertainty around site work in 2026. For you as an investor, the key questions now center on how Oklo executes on permits, construction readiness, and future offtake agreements as the Ohio project progresses.

news_articles.title: Online travel stocks rise after report that OpenAI to scale back direct checkouts
news_articles.content:
Online travel stocks rise after report that OpenAI to scale back direct checkouts
Reuters
Thu, March 5, 2026 at 9:48 AM MST
March 5 (Reuters) - Shares of online travel agencies surged on Thursday after a report that
���is scaling back plans to integrate direct ���bookings into ChatGPT, easing investor fears that the AI chatbots could ���eventually cut out travel intermediaries.
Shares of Expedia were up over 12%, while Booking Holdings and Tripadvisor rose 8% and 5%, respectively.
The rally followed a report by The ���Information that OpenAI ���found ChatGPT users were researching products in the chatbot but not completing purchases through ���it.
The AI company will instead focus on checkouts within specific third-party apps that plug into ChatGPT, the report ���said, citing ���an OpenAI spokesperson.
OpenAI did ���not respond to a ���Reuters request for comment.
Investors and analysts have grown increasingly concerned that generative AI tools could become the dominant platform for planning and booking travel, potentially bypassing intermediaries such as online travel agencies.
We see the OpenAI ���news as incrementally positive for ���online travel agencies, Bernstein analyst ���Richard Clarke said ���in a note.
"This means that Booking and ���Expedia can continue to get ���in front ���of consumers on AI-platforms, lowering the risk of disintermediation," Clarke added.
Expedia and Booking Holdings were among the ���first companies ���to integrate with ChatGPTwhen OpenAI lau

news_articles.title: Stock-Split Watch: Is D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) Next?
Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) Poised to Thrive in an AI-Driven Travel Market
news_articles.content:
Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) Poised to Thrive in an AI-Driven Travel Market
Abdul Rahman
Wed, March 4, 2026 at 5:41 PM MST
Booking Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:BKNG) is one of the
. On February 28, Morgan Stanley argued that online travel agencies (OTAs), with Booking Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:BKNG) as the prime example, are better positioned to benefit from AI than widely feared. This stance directly challenges the consensus view that AI agents will disintermediate traditional booking platforms.
The bank���s key finding is that early agentic AI travel tools are not bypassing OTAs. Instead, they are redirecting users back to their apps and websites to complete bookings. This is because major AI platforms are reluctant to act as merchants of record given the payment risk, customer service burden, refund liabilities, and regulatory obligations involved.
This dynamic preserves Booking���s most critical structural advantage, which is that it remains the merchant of record, said Morgan Stanley. The bank added the Booking retains ownership of the transaction and continues to capture valuable consumer browsing and purchase data. These assets, said Morgan Stanley, are making Booking an indispensable partner in an AI-driven travel world rather than a casualty of it.
In a different update, on February 19, Benchmark cut its price target on Booking to $5,600 from $6,400 while maintaining a Buy rating. The analyst cited valuation concerns even as the company delivered a strong Q4 FY2025 earnings rep

news_articles.title: Booking CFO: The AI Threat Isn’t ‘Really a Risk at All’
news_articles.content:
Booking CFO: The AI Threat Isn���t ���Really a Risk at All���
Dennis Schaal
Tue, March 3, 2026 at 7:05 PM MST
Booking Holdings Chief Financial Officer Ewout Steenbergen said Tuesday the traffic that his company receives from the large language models ���is very small��� and ���it���s not growing.���
���I think that���s, for me, a bit of the surprise over the last few months,��� Steenbergen said at a Morgan Stanley tech conference in San Francisco. ���It���s more or less stable. Some months slightly higher, other months slightly lower. But there���s not really an underlying trend.���
While many investors think the OTAs are about to see travelers abandoning them in droves, Steenbergen said, ���I don���t think disintermediation is really a risk at all.���
On the other hand, the Booking CFO said a potentially ���more realistic risk��� is that Booking would lose some of its direct traffic. In its consumer businesses in 2025, direct traffic to Booking���s platforms was in the mid-60% range.
Steenbergen said travelers are using answer engines ��� such as ChatGPT and Google���s Gemini ��� to do research, and build itineraries, but then they go to websites or apps they trust such as Booking to make the booking.
Our Agentic Tools Will Be As Good
Steenbergen said Booking has two internal startups working on agentic tools that would cover all phases of a trip, including before and after. The goal is that Booking���s agentic tools would be as good as the agentic features on the big horizo

news_articles.content:
Is It Time To Reassess Booking Holdings (BKNG) After Recent Share Price Volatility?
Simply Wall St

with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.
For investors wondering whether Booking Holdings, at around US$4,179.78 per share, still offers value or whether much of the story is already reflected in the price, this article is designed to help frame that question.
The stock has moved around recently, with a 7 day return of 8.0%, a 30 day return showing a 16.4% decline, and a year to date return showing a 21.5% decline, set against a 3 year return of 62.6% and a 5 year return of 79.3%.
Recent headlines have focused on Booking Holdings as a bellwether for global travel demand and on how online travel platforms are responding to changing consumer booking habits. This context helps explain why the market has been reassessing both the risk around the stock and what investors are currently willing to pay for its future prospects.
Simply Wall St currently gives Booking Holdings a
, which means it screens as undervalued on 5 of 6 checks. Next, we break that down across different valuation methods before finishing with a more complete way to think about what the stock might be worth.

news_articles.content:
ServiceNow AI Push Spans Enterprise Telecom And Healthcare As Valuation Lags
Simply Wall St
Thu, March 5, 2026 at 8:10 AM MST
and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.
ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) has launched its Autonomous Workforce AI suite and EmployeeWorks, aimed at coordinating AI specialists to handle complex enterprise workflows end to end.
The company is expanding its AI driven telecom operations through a partnership with NTT DOCOMO and StarHub to support real time autonomous resolution of roaming issues for travelers.
ServiceNow is also partnering with Autonomize AI to develop AI powered healthcare workflow solutions.
ServiceNow enters this product cycle with shares at $113.86 and a mixed recent track record, including a 9.2% gain over the past week but a 22.8% decline year to date and a 37.8% decline over the past year. Over a longer view, returns of 27.4% over three years and 20.1% over five years highlight how the stock has moved through different market phases.
For you as an investor, this cluster of new launches and partnerships shows how ServiceNow is positioning its platform across enterprise, telecom, and healthcare workflows. The key questions from here are how quickly customers adopt these AI offerings and how effectively the company turns that adoption into durable revenue and margin contributions.

news_articles.title: Investors Heavily Search ServiceNow, Inc. (NOW): Here is What You Need to Know
news_articles.content:
Investors Heavily Search ServiceNow, Inc. (NOW): Here is What You Need to Know
Zacks Equity Research
Thu, March 5, 2026 at 7:00 AM MST
ServiceNow
(NOW) has been one of the most searched-for stocks on Zacks.com lately. So, you might want to look at some of the facts that could shape the stock's performance in the near term.
Shares of this maker of software that automates companies' technology operations have returned +2.5% over the past month versus the Zacks S&P 500 composite's -0.2% change. The Zacks Computers - IT Services industry, to which ServiceNow belongs, has lost 2.3% over this period. Now the key question is: Where could the stock be headed in the near term?
While media releases or rumors about a substantial change in a company's business prospects usually make its stock 'trending' and lead to an immediate price change, there are always some fundamental facts that eventually dominate the buy-and-hold decision-making.
Earnings Estimate Revisions
Rather than focusing on anything else, we at Zacks prioritize evaluating the change in a company's earnings projection. This is because we believe the fair value for its stock is determined by the present value of its future stream of earnings.
We essentially look at how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to reflect the impact of the latest business trends. And if earnings estimates go up for a company, the fair value for its stock goes up. A higher fair value than the current market p

Long story short, there is a near-perfect correlation between consistent earnings growth and huge winners. Taking that into account, here are three market-beating stocks with room for further growth.

news_articles.content:
Dan Jablonsky Joins BWXT Board As Investors Weigh Growth And Valuation
Simply Wall St
Wed, March 4, 2026 at 5:26 PM MST
with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.
BWX Technologies (NYSE:BWXT) has appointed Dan Jablonsky to its Board of Directors.
Jablonsky brings aerospace, defense, and leadership experience, including prior responsibility for guiding Maxar Technologies through an acquisition.
His background includes technical and operational roles linked to the U.S. Navy.
For you as an investor, BWX Technologies sits at the intersection of nuclear technology, defense, and government-focused programs. These areas tend to rely heavily on long contract cycles and specialized expertise. Board-level changes at a company with this profile can shape how it pursues long term opportunities, manages risk, and allocates capital across its core lines of business.
Jablonsky���s mix of corporate leadership and technical experience gives BWXT an additional voice familiar with complex programs, regulatory demands, and large customer relationships. As the company evaluates future projects, potential partnerships, or M&A options, his input on the board may influence how BWXT weighs growth, balance sheet discipline, and execution risk.

OPXS Skyrockets 140% in a Year: Should You Buy the Stock?
Debasmita Chatterjee
Wed, March 4, 2026 at 10:27 AM MST
Optex Systems Holdings, Inc.
OPXS shares have surged 139.5% in the past year compared with the industry���s 46.2% growth. The company has outperformed other industry players, including
BWX Technologies, Inc.
BWXT and
Axon Enterprise, Inc.
AXON. Shares of BWXT and AXON have rallied 98% and 8.2%, respectively, in the same time frame. OPXS benefits from strong defense demand, a growing order backlog, long-term relationships with major contractors, and specialized expertise in precision optical systems for military platforms.
Zacks Investment Research
A Key Look Into OPXS��� Business Operations
Optex Systems manufactures optical sighting systems and related assemblies for the U.S. Department of Defense, foreign military programs, commercial markets and consumer applications. Its products are used on U.S. military land vehicles such as Abrams, Bradley, Stryker and other armored vehicles, and include periscopes, rifle and surveillance sights, night vision assemblies and other optical components.
Most products are built to customer specifications and delivered directly to the armed services or defense prime contractors, while less than 1% of revenue comes from reselling components originally produced by the company. It also secured several new defense contracts for optical systems, laser filters and periscopes supporting U.S. and allied military programs. Its products ar

news_articles.title: American Superconductor, BWX, Redwire, Corning, and SolarEdge Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
news_articles.content:
American Superconductor, BWX, Redwire, Corning, and SolarEdge Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
Petr Hu��큐찼k
Tue, March 3, 2026 at 1:37 PM MST
What Happened?
A number of stocks fell in the afternoon session after geopolitical tensions in the Middle East sent crude oil prices soaring, stoking fears of resurgent inflation.
The price for Brent crude, the international benchmark, leaped over 6% to $82.57 a barrel amid an escalating war with Iran, which has threatened to block the Strait of Hormuz. This critical waterway handles about 20% of global oil flow. A sustained increase in energy prices could translate to higher inflation, potentially impacting consumer spending and corporate earnings. This scenario also complicates the Federal Reserve's path forward, as persistent inflation could delay anticipated interest rate cuts that investors have been counting on to support the economy.
The stock market overreacts to news, and big price drops can present good opportunities to buy high-quality stocks.
Among others, the following stocks were impacted:
Renewable Energy company American Superconductor (NASDAQ:AMSC) fell 5.7%. Is now the time to buy American Superconductor?
Defense Contractors company BWX (NYSE:BWXT) fell 4.6%. Is now the time to buy BWX?
Aerospace company Redwire (NYSE:RDW) fell 6.1%. Is now the time to buy Redwire?
Electronic Components company Corning (NYSE:GLW) fell 4.9%. Is now the time to buy Corning?
Renewable Energy company SolarEdge (NASDAQ:SEDG) fell 5.

news_articles.title: Canada and India Sign Landmark Uranium Deal Worth $2.6B
news_articles.content:
Canada and India Sign Landmark Uranium Deal Worth $2.6B
Andrew Topf
Wed, March 4, 2026 at 10:00 AM MST
Ever since Donald Trump slapped tariffs on Canadian goods on Feb. 1, 2025, Prime Minister Mark Carney has been encouraging trade with nations other than the United States.
The former central banker turned politician wants to double non-US exports over the next decade.
Towards that goal, Carney met with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi in Delhi on Monday as part of a four-day trip to deepen trade and diplomatic ties.
The centerpiece was a deal between the Indian government and Saskatchewan-based uranium producer Cameco (TSX:CCO) to supply nearly 22 million pounds of uranium for nuclear energy generation between 2027 and 2035.
Also, British Columbia coal producer Elk Valley Resources — majority-owned by Glencore (LSE:GLEN) — will sell 1.2 million tonnes of coal to India worth hundreds of millions of dollars. (
)
As
newspaper,
Emerging from a set of meetings with India Prime Minister Narendra Modi earlier in the day, Carney announced that a new $2.6-billion agreement had been struck between India and Saskatchewan that will see the Prairie province supply it with uranium, which India needs for nuclear power generation.
The 10-year deal, set to begin in 2027, is part of what the Prime Minister’s Office calls a new “strategic energy partnership,” which was one of the outcomes expected out of Canada’s renewed interest in working with India.
The uranium contract with Saskatoon-ba

Shares of this industrial conglomerate have returned +10.2% over the past month versus the Zacks S&P 500 composite's -0.2% change. The Zacks Aerospace - Defense industry, to which GE belongs, has gained 7.2% over this period. Now the key question is: Where could the stock be headed in the near term?
While media releases or rumors about a substantial change in a company's business prospects usually make its stock 'trending' and lead to an immediate price change, there are always some fundamental facts that eventually dominate the buy-and-hold decision-making.
Earnings Estimate Revisions
Rather than focusing on anything else, we at Zacks prioritize evaluating the change in a company's earnings projection. This is because we believe the fair value for its stock is determined by the present value of its future stream of earnings.
Our analysis is essentially based on how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to take the latest business trends into account. When earnings estimates for a company go up, the fair value for its stock goes up as well. And when a stock's fair value is higher than its current market price, invest

news_articles.title: Intelligent Alpha CEO’s Top Mag-7 Picks: Google and Apple for Personal AI
news_articles.content:
The CEO of Intelligent Alpha has a clear thesis for 2026: personal AI is the dominant theme, and two Mag-7 giants are best positioned to own it.
“You have to look at Google and Apple. And for a very specific reason. It’s personal AI, which I think will be the biggest theme in AI this year. Personal AI is an AI that you think of it as the Siri that we all wish we had.”
That quote cuts right to the heart of it. Not the AI that writes your emails or generates images. The AI that knows you, lives on your device, and becomes the operating layer between you and everything else. The Siri we all wish we had.
Why Google and Apple Win Personal AI
Alphabet
(
|
) has the data infrastructure and the model quality. The Gemini app now has 750M+ monthly active users and processes over 10 billion tokens per minute. Google’s search business generated $63.07 billion in Q4 2025, and CEO Sundar Pichai framed it well on the earnings call: “Search saw more usage than ever before, with AI continuing to drive an expansionary moment.” Google Cloud is now running at an annualized rate of over $70 billion. The company is committing $175 to $185 billion in CapEx for 2026, nearly doubling last year’s spend. That is not a company hedging its bets.
Apple
(
) has something Google cannot easily replicate: the device. With 2.5 billion+ active devices in the installed base and iPhone posting its best quarter ever at $85.27 billion, Apple sits at the intersection of hardware, software, and personal data in a way

Shares of

(LRCX) have gained 6.3% over the past four weeks to close the last trading session at $222.99, but there could still be a solid upside left in the stock if short-term price targets of Wall Street analysts are any indication. Going by the price targets, the mean estimate of $279.69 indicates a potential upside of 25.4%.
The mean estimate comprises 28 short-term price targets with a standard deviation of $23.99. While the lowest estimate of $221.00 indicates a 0.9% decline from the current price level, the most optimistic analyst expects the stock to surge 45.8% to reach $325.00. It's very important to note the standard deviation here, as it helps understand the variability of the estimates. The smaller the standard deviation, the greater the agreement among analysts.
While the consensus price target is highly sought after by investors, the ability and unbiasedness of analysts in setting price targets have long been questionable. And investors making investment decisions solely based on this tool would arguably do themselves a disservice.
However, an impressive consensus price target is not the only factor that indicates a potential upside in LRCX. This view is strengthened by the agreement among analysts that the company will report better earnings than what they estimated earlier. Though a positive tre

news_articles.title: Lam Research (LRCX) Crossed Above the 50-Day Moving Average: What That Means for Investors
news_articles.content:
Lam Research (LRCX) Crossed Above the 50-Day Moving Average: What That Means for Investors
Zacks Equity Research
Thu, March 5, 2026 at 7:30 AM MST
Lam Research (LRCX) is looking like an interesting pick from a technical perspective, as the company reached a key level of support. Recently, LRCX broke out above the 50-day moving average, suggesting a short-term bullish trend.
The 50-day simple moving average is one of three major moving averages used by traders and analysts to determine support or resistance levels for a wide range of securities. But the 50-day is considered to be more important because it's the first marker of an up or down trend.
LRCX has rallied 6.3% over the past four weeks, and the company is a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) at the moment. This combination suggests LRCX could be on the verge of another move higher.
The bullish case solidifies once investors consider LRCX's positive earnings estimate revisions. No estimate has gone lower in the past two months for the current fiscal year, compared to 11 higher, while the consensus estimate has increased too.
Given this move in earnings estimate revisions and the positive technical factor, investors may want to keep their eye on LRCX for more gains in the near future.

ASML Holding, KLA Corporation and Lam Research shares have appreciated 25%, 20.5% and 37%, respectively, in the past three months. Given the rise of AMAT stock in the past three months and its dynamics with the other wafer fabrication equipment (WFE) players, investors are wondering: Is it the right time to buy, sell or hold AMAT stock?
AMAT Leads the WFE Space With a Broad Portfolio
The wafer fabrication equipment supply chain has multiple fabrication steps, and AMAT has the broadest and most diversified offerings as it provides solutions across multiple fabrication steps, like deposition, materials engineering, etch, metrology and packaging. While its competitors serve only one or two verticals.
For instance, Applied Materials and KLA Corporation offer similar solutions like Wafer Inspection, Yield Enhancement and Process Control inspection systems. Lam Research develops Atomic Layer Deposition tools like AT200M, AT410 and AT650P that are similar to the devices made by AMAT.
Both ASML and AMAT specialize in advanced semiconductor nodes, al

news_articles.title: ASML's Revenue Mix is Changing: What are the Latest Growth Drivers?
news_articles.content:
ASML's Revenue Mix is Changing: What are the Latest Growth Drivers?
Subham Roy
Thu, March 5, 2026 at 7:23 AM MST
ASML Holding
ASML completed its 2025 results with ���32.67 billion, up 15.6% year over year. ASML���s net systems sales contributed 74.9% of the top line, up 12.4% year over year. ASML���s net systems sales saw a decline in contribution from China. China contributed 41% to net systems sales in 2024 and 33% in 2025.
The decline was mainly due to U.S.-led export restrictions combined with demand normalization following high deep ultraviolet (DUV) equipment demand in 2024. However, the gap was filled by South Korea and Taiwan, which contributed 25% and 22% in 2025, up from 21% and 11%, respectively, in the year-ago quarter.
On the technology side, extreme ultraviolet (EUV) technology is expanding its contribution to the top line, replacing DUV. EUV contributed 48% to the net systems sales, up from the contribution of 38% in 2024. The demand for EUV is rising proportionately with the growing usage of AI chips, as foundries have to transition to 3nm / 2nm nodes requiring more EUV layers per chip.
The rise in demand for memory products like high bandwidth memory and DDR is also causing traction in EUV technology. As the world moves toward sub-2nm manufacturing, the demand for High-NA EUV systems will also rise, giving ASML a multi-year runway for harnessing growth, as evident in the ���38.8 billion backlog in the year ended 2025.
How Competitors Fare Against ASML
ASML co

(MSTR) have gained 13.4% over the past four weeks to close the last trading session at $146.44, but there could still be a solid upside left in the stock if short-term price targets of Wall Street analysts are any indication. Going by the price targets, the mean estimate of $395.93 indicates a potential upside of 170.4%.
The average comprises 14 short-term price targets ranging from a low of $185.00 to a high of $705.00, with a standard deviation of $172.27. While the lowest estimate indicates an increase of 26.3% from the current price level, the most optimistic estimate points to a 381.4% upside. More than the range, one should note the standard deviation here, as it helps understand the variability of the estimates. The smaller the standard deviation, the greater the agreement among analysts.
While the consensus price target is a much-coveted metric for investors, solely banking on this metric to make an investment decision may not be wise at all. That's because the ability and unbiasedness of analysts in setting price targets have long been questionable.
However, an impressive consensus price target is not the only factor that indicates a potential upside in MSTR. This view is strengthened by the agreement among analysts that the company will report better earnings than what they estimated earlier. Though

. In Q4 2025, Patient Opportunity Equity Strategy returned 5.99% net of fees, compared to its unmanaged benchmark, the S&P 500 Index, which returned 2.65%. The market ended a strong year with 17.9% returns, marking the seventh-best three-year period in market history. 2025 was a year of AI, extending the benefits to hardware, energy, and component suppliers across the ecosystem. Seven stocks accounted for over half of the S&P 500���s returns, posing difficulties for active managers. Nevertheless, the strategy performed well, outperforming the S&P 500 for the third consecutive year with a return of 26.1%.
